reasoning: The market and empirical evidence strongly indicate that Elon Musk will not step down as Tesla CEO by the end of 2025. Despite facing calls to resign earlier in 2025 from some investors and employees due to performance issues, Musk publicly committed in May 2025 to staying as CEO for at least another five years. Furthermore, he scaled back his US government-related duties to dedicate more time to Tesla. Recent reporting from October 2025 shows that his net worth surged past $500 billion driven by Tesla stock performance, further entrenching his position. The remaining timeline for a departure to occur in 2025 is very short (just over 2 months), making an abrupt exit highly unlikely absent an unforeseen scandal or catastrophic health event. The baseline risk of sudden departure over a 2-month period is exceptionally low. I am setting the probability to the minimum allowed threshold of 5%.
